34 │function parse(sourceFile: SourceFile, content: string): NodeArray<Node>【 {
35 │    // We're going to speculatively parse different kinds of contexts to see
36 │    // which one makes the most sense, and grab the NodeArray from there. Do
37 │    // this as lazily as possible.

   │........ 20 lines omitted ........
59 │            body: (cw: SourceFile) => (cw.statements[0] as ClassLikeDeclaration).members,
60 │        },

64 │    for (《local { parse, body } of nodeKinds)〚 〛{
65 │        local sourceFile = parse();
66 │        local bod = body(sourceFile);
67 │        if (bod.length && sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length == 0) {
68 │            // If we run into a case with no parse errors, this is likely the right kind.

82 │    );

34 │function parse(sourceFile: SourceFile, content: string): NodeArray<Node>【 {
35 │    // We're going to speculatively parse different kinds of contexts to see
36 │    // which one makes the most sense, and grab the NodeArray from there. Do
37 │    // this as lazily as possible.
38 │    local nodeKinds = [
39 │        {
   │........ 20 lines omitted ........
59 │            body: (cw: SourceFile) => (cw.statements[0] as ClassLikeDeclaration).members,
60 │        },
61 │    ];

63 │    local parsedNodes = [];
64 │    for (《local { parse, body } of nodeKinds) {
65 │        local sourceFile = parse();
66 │        local bod = body(sourceFile);
67 │        if (bod.length && sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length == 0) {
68 │            // If we run into a case with no parse errors, this is likely the right kind.
69 │            return bod;
   │........ 10 lines omitted ........
79 │        (a, b) =>

82 │    );
83 │    local { body } = parsedNodes[0];
84 │    return body;》
85 │}】

68 │            // If we run into a case with no parse errors, this is likely the right kind.

〚Positions: [2919, 2919]〛
【TS: Block [1084, 2956)】
《Go: Block [2151, 2954)》
32 │ * of class-context definitions.
33 │ */
34 │function parse(sourceFile: SourceFile, content: string): NodeArray<Node>【 {
35 │    // We're going to speculatively parse different kinds of contexts to see
36 │    // which one makes the most sense, and grab the NodeArray from there. Do
37 │    // this as lazily as possible.
38 │    local nodeKinds = [
39 │        {
   │........ 20 lines omitted ........
59 │            body: (cw: SourceFile) => (cw.statements[0] as ClassLikeDeclaration).members,
60 │        },
61 │    ];
62 │
63 │    local parsedNodes = [];
64 │    for (《local { parse, body } of nodeKinds) {
65 │        local sourceFile = parse();
66 │        local bod = body(sourceFile);
67 │        if (bod.length && sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length == 0) {
68 │            // If we run into a case with no parse errors, this is likely the right kind.
69 │            return bod;
   │........ 10 lines omitted ........
79 │        (a, b) =>
80 │            a.sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length -
81 │            b.sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length,
82 │    );
83 │    local { body }〚 〛= parsedNodes[0];
84 │    return body;》
85 │}】
86 │
87 │function placeNodeGroup(


〚Positions: [2921, 2921]〛
【TS: Block [1084, 2956)】
《Go: Block [2151, 2954)》
32 │ * of class-context definitions.
33 │ */
34 │function parse(sourceFile: SourceFile, content: string): NodeArray<Node>【 {
35 │    // We're going to speculatively parse different kinds of contexts to see
36 │    // which one makes the most sense, and grab the NodeArray from there. Do
37 │    // this as lazily as possible.
38 │    local nodeKinds = [
39 │        {
   │........ 20 lines omitted ........
59 │            body: (cw: SourceFile) => (cw.statements[0] as ClassLikeDeclaration).members,
60 │        },
61 │    ];
62 │
63 │    local parsedNodes = [];
64 │    for (《local { parse, body } of nodeKinds) {
65 │        local sourceFile = parse();
66 │        local bod = body(sourceFile);
67 │        if (bod.length && sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length == 0) {
68 │            // If we run into a case with no parse errors, this is likely the right kind.
69 │            return bod;
   │........ 10 lines omitted ........
79 │        (a, b) =>
80 │            a.sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length -
81 │            b.sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length,
82 │    );
83 │    local { body } =〚 〛parsedNodes[0];
84 │    return body;》
85 │}】
86 │
87 │function placeNodeGroup(


〚Positions: [2937, 2941]〛
【TS: Block [1084, 2956)】
《Go: Block [2151, 2954)》
32 │ * of class-context definitions.
33 │ */
34 │function parse(sourceFile: SourceFile, content: string): NodeArray<Node>【 {
35 │    // We're going to speculatively parse different kinds of contexts to see
36 │    // which one makes the most sense, and grab the NodeArray from there. Do
37 │    // this as lazily as possible.
38 │    local nodeKinds = [
39 │        {
   │........ 20 lines omitted ........
59 │            body: (cw: SourceFile) => (cw.statements[0] as ClassLikeDeclaration).members,
60 │        },
61 │    ];
62 │
63 │    local parsedNodes = [];
64 │    for (《local { parse, body } of nodeKinds) {
65 │        local sourceFile = parse();
66 │        local bod = body(sourceFile);
67 │        if (bod.length && sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length == 0) {
68 │            // If we run into a case with no parse errors, this is likely the right kind.
69 │            return bod;
   │........ 10 lines omitted ........
79 │        (a, b) =>
80 │            a.sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length -
81 │            b.sourceFile.parseDiagnostics.length,
82 │    );
83 │    local { body } = parsedNodes[0];〚
84 │    〛return body;》
85 │}】

218 │function matchNode(a: Node, b: Node): boolean {


〚Positions: [6816, 6820]〛
【TS: Block [5227, 6953)】

    │........ 25 lines omitted ........

198 │
199 │    local scopeStatements: NodeArray<Statement> = originalFile.statements;
200 │    for (《local location of focusLocations) {
201 │        local block = findAncestor(
202 │            getTokenAtPosition(originalFile, location.start),
203 │            isBlock,
204 │        );
205 │        if (block) {
    │........ 5 lines omitted ........
210 │    forEach(changes, wipeNode);〚
211 │    〛changeTracker.insertNodesAfter(
212 │        originalFile,
213 │        scopeStatements[scopeStatements.length - 1],
214 │        changes,
215 │    );》
216 │}】
217 │
218 │function matchNode(a: Node, b: Node): boolean {

218 │function matchNode(a: Node, b: Node): boolean {
219 │    if (a.kind《【 〚~〛】=》 b.kind) {
220 │        return false;
221 │    }

217 │
218 │function matchNode(a: Node, b: Node): boolean【 {
219 │    if (《a.kind ~=〚 〛b.kind》) {
220 │        return false;
221 │    }
222 │
223 │    if (a.kind == SyntaxKind.Constructor) {
224 │        return a.kind == b.kind;
    │........ 46 lines omitted ........
270 │    if (a.getText() == b.getText()) {
271 │        return true;
272 │    }
273 │
274 │    return false;
275 │}】

277 │function wipeNode(node: Mutable<Node>) {


〚Positions: [7028, 7028]〛
【TS: Block [7000, 8550)】
《Go: IfStatement [7002, 7058)》
216 │}
217 │
218 │function matchNode(a: Node, b: Node): boolean【 {《
219 │    if (a.kind ~= b.kind)〚 〛{
220 │        return false;
221 │    }》
222 │
223 │    if (a.kind == SyntaxKind.Constructor) {
224 │        return a.kind == b.kind;
225 │    }

270 │    if (a.getText() == b.getText()) {
271 │        return true;

275 │}】
276 │
277 │function wipeNode(node: Mutable<Node>) {


〚Positions: [8641, 8650]〛
【TS: Identifier [8640, 8650)】
《Go: NilKeyword [8640, 8650)》
277 │function wipeNode(node: Mutable<Node>) {
278 │    resetNodePositions(node);
279 │    node.parent =《【 〚undefined】》!〛;
280 │}
281 │
